title: malus × riversicarnea
intro: Malus × riversicarnea, also known as the Rivers' crabapple, is a hybrid plant species resulting from the cross between Malus baccata (Siberian crabapple) and Malus domestica (domestic apple). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Malus × riversicarnea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anticancer properties. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to address various health concerns and promote overall well-being.
